objectives.text: Report your Chapel and Ash Road conduct to Father Augustine.
Pay or pray through Father Augustine's road absolution.

stages.description: Father Augustine says Chapel work and Ash Road work both leave marks. The Censure writes those marks down before sending anyone toward Hallowfen.
Father Augustine has read the road record back to you. Absolution costs five ducats, or the missing coin made up in prayers.
You paid Father Augustine's road price. Your soul now has a stamped receipt.
You made up the missing ducats with prayers. Father Augustine counted each one like a man auditing rain.
You lied about your purse. Father Augustine noticed, charged the extra ducat, and stamped the chit anyway.
You invoked the field canon for service performed under writ. Augustine entered no fee and stamped the chit.
You traded Augustine a clean field account for the fee. He kept the testimony and stamped the chit.
You buried Augustine's fee in a dead-letter entry. The chit is valid until someone reads the ledger closely.
